name: Zuhayr ibn Sulaym
title: Azdi Warrior
shortDescription: A man from the Azd tribe who realized the Imam's righteousness on the night of Ashura, defected from Umar Sa'd's army, and joined the Imam.
fullStory:
Zuhayr ibn Sulaym al-Azdi was a warrior from the prominent Azd tribe. Initially, he came to Karbala from Kufa with Umar Sa'd's army to fight against Imam Hussain (AS).

On the night of Ashura, when Umar Sa'd and Ibn Ziyad rejected all of Imam Hussain's (AS) peaceful proposals and it became clear they definitely intended to shed the blood of the Prophet's (PBUH) son, Zuhayr underwent a profound transformation. His awakened conscience did not allow him to remain in the army of falsehood.

He used the darkness of the night to secretly leave Umar Sa'd's camp and join Imam Hussain's (AS) luminous camp. The Imam welcomed his repentance with open arms.

On the day of Ashura, Zuhayr ibn Sulaym, who had now become a defender of Wilayah, raised the flag of defense when the enemy's first attack began. He fought valiantly until he was martyred in that all-out attack, achieving a blessed end.
quoteArabic: السَّلامُ عَلى زُهَيْرِ بْنِ سُلَيْمٍ الأَزْدِيِّ...
quote: (From Ziyarat Nahiya:) Peace be upon Zuhayr ibn Sulaym al-Azdi.
